description: ROUND 4 - Skills Are Never Actually Loaded: The skill system has 27 registered skills with compose_for_phase() infrastructure but NOTHING populates state.context.skills_loaded. The orchestrator does not select skills, and context_assembler only loads descriptions (not actual SKILL.md content). Fix by adding skill auto-selection and proper content loading.
steps: Add select_skills_for_goal(goal: str, available_skills: list[dict]) -> list[str] function to kernel/skill_selector.py (new file). This analyzes goal text against skill tags/descriptions using keyword matching: extract keywords from goal, match against skill tags and description words, return top N skills (default 5) sorted by relevance score.
Update kernel/prompts/orchestrator.md to include: 'After loading the goal, select relevant skills using tag matching and write them to state.context.skills_loaded'

Update runner.py: in the init phase (when current_node is init and in dry-run/Mode 1), auto-select skills for the goal and write to state.context.skills_loaded. Import skill_selector and KnowledgeStore.

Fix context_assembler.py _load_skills method: instead of just loading descriptions, use SkillComposer.compose(skill_names) to load the actual SKILL.md content for each skill. Pass a max_tokens budget (default 4000) to avoid bloating the prompt.
Add --skills CLI flag to runner.py parse_args for manual skill override (comma-separated list). When provided, use these instead of auto-selection.

Write tests in tests/test_skill_selector.py: select_skills_for_goal returns relevant skills for coding goals, design goals, etc. Empty goal returns empty list. Skills with matching tags score higher. max_skills parameter works.
Update existing tests if needed to accommodate the new skill loading behavior
acceptance_criteria: Function correctly matches goal keywords against skill tags and descriptions
runner.py auto-selects skills during init and stores in state.context.skills_loaded
context_assembler loads actual SKILL.md content (not just descriptions) via SkillComposer
--skills CLI flag works for manual override
findings: All 405 tests pass at 98.39% coverage. Created kernel/skill_selector.py with select_skills_for_goal function using tag-weighted keyword matching (tags 3x, description 1x). Updated runner.py with --skills CLI flag and auto-selection after workspace init. Updated context_assembler._load_skills to use SkillComposer.compose() with fallback to descriptions. Updated orchestrator.md with skill selection instruction. Added 20 new tests in tests/test_skill_selector.py covering all acceptance criteria.
